hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will improve SSTR2 selectivity by forming favorable interactions with SSTR2-unique ECL2 residues (e.g., 192/193/195/197), while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) electrostatic complementarity while being tolerated by SSTR2.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving strong SSTR2 binding but are not sufficiently disrupting off-target interactions. Targeting non-pharmacophore positions for mutagenesis and tightening the ddG threshold will help improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: To target non-pharmacophore positions where off-target interactions (especially with SSTR3/SSTR5) are likely occurring.
The current ddG threshold may be too lenient, allowing candidates with strong off-target binding. Tightening the threshold will filter out weaker binders and prioritize more selective designs.
